name: Grpc

unions.name: Error
unions.comment:
A gRPC request may fail with in a variety of ways.
Since the request is just a HTTP request under the hood, it may fail in the roughly same ways a HTTP request could.

  - `BadUrl` means you did not provide a valid URL.

  - `Timeout` means it took too long to get a response.

  - `NetworkError` means the user turned off their wifi, went in a cave, etc.

  - `BadStatus` means you got a response back, but the http status code indicates failure. This can be either
    a bad HTTP status code or a bad `grpc-status` response header according to
    <https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/http-grpc-status-mapping.md>

  - `BadBody` means you got a response back with a nice status code, but the body
    of the response was something unexpected. The `Bytes` in this case is the response that failed to be parsed.

  - `UnknownGrpcStatus` means you got a response back but the `grpc-status` header was set to either

```
  - something that is not an `Int`
  - an `Int` that is outside of the range of the `ErrCode` enum.

Note that not setting the header is fine and will delegate failure checking to the HTTP status.
```
cases: { metadata : Http.Metadata, response : Bytes.Bytes, errMessage : String.String, status : Grpc.GrpcStatus }

unions.name: GrpcStatus
unions.comment:
A valid `grpc-status` header encodes one of these possibilities as an Int.

  - `0` -> Ok\_
  - `1` -> Cancelled
  - `2` -> Unknown
  - ...

aliases.name: Rpc
aliases.comment:
Opaque type for an rpc endpoint. These should be constructed by the `protoc-gen-elm` code generator.
If for some reason, you need to define an endpoint by hand, you can do so by importing the `Grpc.Internal` module.

aliases.name: RpcRequest
aliases.comment:
A request to the server that has not been sent yet. This is designed to be used in a builder-pattern kind of way:

    Grpc.new helloWorld "hello"
        |> Grpc.addHeader "authorization" token
        |> Grpc.setHost "http://example.com"

    In this example `helloWorld` could be a value of type `Rpc String String` that was generated by `protoc-gen-elm`.

values.name: addHeader
values.comment: Add a header to a request. The first parameter is the header name, the second parameter is the value.
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> Grpc.InternalRpcRequest req res usesTracker -> Grpc.InternalRpcRequest req res usesTracker

values.name: addHeaders
values.comment: Add multiple headers at once to a request.
values.type: List.List ( String.String, String.String ) -> Grpc.InternalRpcRequest req res usesTracker -> Grpc.InternalRpcRequest req res usesTracker

values.name: new
values.comment: Build a request from an rpc endpoint by providing a request body.
values.type: Grpc.Rpc req res -> req -> Grpc.RpcRequest req res

values.name: setHost
values.comment: Set the host for the request. By default the host is empty (i.e. the current website), which is recommended to avoid CORS issues.
values.type: String.String -> Grpc.InternalRpcRequest req res usesTracker -> Grpc.InternalRpcRequest req res usesTracker

values.name: setTimeout
values.comment: Make the request cancel itself after a given timeout in milliseconds.
values.type: Basics.Float -> Grpc.InternalRpcRequest req res usesTracker -> Grpc.InternalRpcRequest req res usesTracker

values.name: setTracker
values.comment:
The tracker lets you cancel and track requests via `Http.cancel` and `Http.track`.
This only works if you use `toCmd` to send your Request, Tasks unfortunately do not support this.
We use the type system to our advantage here to prevent you from calling `toTask` after using this function.
values.type: String.String -> Grpc.InternalRpcRequest req res usesTracker -> Grpc.InternalRpcRequest req res ()

values.name: toCmd
values.comment: Convert the given `RpcRequest` into a Command to make the Elm runtime execute the request.
values.type: (Result.Result Grpc.Error res -> msg) -> Grpc.InternalRpcRequest req res usesTracker -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: toTask
values.comment:
Convert the given `RpcRequest` into a Task. This can be useful if you want to chain multiple requests but are only
interested in the end result.
values.type: Grpc.RpcRequest req res -> Task.Task Grpc.Error res

values.name: withRisk
values.comment: Make the request "risky" (i.e. "withCredentials=true" in javascript). This allows Set-Cookie to function in gRPC responses.
values.type: Grpc.InternalRpcRequest req res usesTracker -> Grpc.InternalRpcRequest req res usesTracker

name: Grpc.Internal

unions.name: Rpc
cases: { service : String.String, package : String.String, rpcName : String.String, encoder : req -> Protobuf.Encode.Encoder, decoder : Protobuf.Decode.Decoder res }
